description: Fix 'Error: spawn EINVAL' that crashes the server on Windows. The root cause is passing environment objects with undefined values to child_process.spawn/fork, which libuv rejects on Windows. Also fix bare 'npm' commands that skip platformExec(), missing result.error checks on spawnSync, and add a sanitizeEnv helper to strip undefined values from env objects before spawning.

userStories.title: Add sanitizeEnv utility to shared cross-platform module
userStories.description: Add a sanitizeEnv function to platform/shared/src/cross-platform.ts that strips undefined values from environment objects, making them safe to pass to spawn/fork on all platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: sanitizeEnv returns a new Record<string, string> with all undefined-valued keys removed
sanitizeEnv preserves all string-valued entries unchanged

userStories.title: Fix spawn EINVAL in CLI start command (foreground and background modes)
userStories.description: Use sanitizeEnv in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts to strip undefined env values before passing to spawn, fixing the crash that Windows users hit when running 'opentabs start'.
acceptanceCriteria: env object passed to spawn in both foreground (line 417) and background (line 359) modes is sanitized
No env value is undefined when passed to spawn
The cast `as NodeJS.ProcessEnv` is removed — sanitizeEnv returns the correct type
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (fix env handling)

Context:
The current code at line 314:
```ts
const env: Record<string, string | undefined> = { ...process.env };
env.PORT = String(port);
```

This env object is used in two places:
1. Line 359-362 (background mode): `spawn(platformExec('node'), [serverEntry, ...serverArgs], { env: env as NodeJS.ProcessEnv, ... })`
2. Line 417-420 (foreground mode via spawnStreaming): `spawnStreaming(platformExec('node'), [serverEntry, ...serverArgs], { env: env, ... })` which passes to `spawn` at line 40-43 with `env: opts?.env as NodeJS.ProcessEnv`

Changes:
1. Add import: `import { DEFAULT_PORT, isWindows, platformExec, sanitizeEnv, toErrorMessage } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';`
2. Change line 314 from:
```ts
const env: Record<string, string | undefined> = { ...process.env };
```
to:
```ts
const env: Record<string, string> = sanitizeEnv({ ...process.env, PORT: String(port) });
```
3. Remove the separate `env.PORT = String(port);` assignment on line 315 (now inline above)
4. At line 359-362, remove `as NodeJS.ProcessEnv` cast — sanitizeEnv already returns Record<string, string>:
```ts
const child = spawn(platformExec('node'), [serverEntry, ...serverArgs], {
  env: env,
  stdio: ['ignore', logStream, logStream],
  detached: true,
});
```
5. Also update the spawnStreaming type signature at line 38. Change:
```ts
opts?: { cwd?: string; env?: Record<string, string | undefined>; stdin?: 'inherit' | 'pipe' | 'ignore' },
```
to:
```ts
opts?: { cwd?: string; env?: Record<string, string>; stdin?: 'inherit' | 'pipe' | 'ignore' },
```
6. And update line 42 to remove the cast:
```ts
env: opts?.env,
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other behavior in start.ts
- The PORT env var must still be set correctly
- Keep the spawnStreaming function signature compatible with all its callers (only one caller at line 417)

userStories.title: Fix spawn EINVAL in dev-proxy fork call
userStories.description: Use sanitizeEnv in platform/mcp-server/src/dev-proxy.ts to strip undefined env values before passing to fork.
acceptanceCriteria: env object passed to fork at line 222 is sanitized
No env value is undefined when passed to fork
skipPermissionsOverride logic still works correctly (empty string for false, '1' for true)

userStories.title: Fix bare 'npm' in scaffold.ts and missing result.error checks in update.ts
userStories.description: Fix scaffold.ts using bare 'npm' instead of platformExec('npm'), and add result.error checks to spawnSync calls in update.ts that would throw on Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: scaffold.ts queryNpmRegistryVersion uses platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm'
update.ts getLatestVersion checks result.error before accessing stdout/stderr
update.ts performUpdate checks result.error before accessing result.status
update.ts restartBackgroundServer checks result.error before accessing result.status

userStories.title: Add EINVAL-specific error messaging to CLI spawn helpers
userStories.description: Add EINVAL detection to the CLI's spawn helper functions so users get an actionable error message instead of a raw stack trace when spawn fails with EINVAL.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnProcessAsync in plugin.ts catches spawn errors with code EINVAL and wraps with actionable message
spawnInherit in plugin.ts catches spawn errors with code EINVAL and wraps with actionable message
spawnStreaming in start.ts provides EINVAL-specific error handling via its error reject path
Error message mentions Windows and suggests checking environment variables
